methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Base Power is a Series A climate and energy technology company founded by Zach Dell and Justin Lopas that has raised over $50M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of nine private companies and one public entity (LNZA) operating in the same sector and stage. The primary peer set consists of four same-stage climate and energy companies: Watershed ($1.8B), Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach, using total raised ($50M) as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, implies a $100M valuation. The peer-median method across the four same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $175M, with a range of $110M to $1.8B. The wide range reflects material dispersion in the comp set, with Watershed commanding a substantial premium relative to the other three peers.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $85M to $201M, with a median point of $138M. Key uncertainties include the appropriate positioning of Base Power within the peer spread—whether it tracks more closely to the $110M–$150M cluster or toward Watershed's elevated valuation—and the relevance of public comps in a rapidly evolving sector. The $138M midpoint reflects the balance of these two methods but carries material tail risk given peer dispersion.
